name: GCC-789 (GCC Series #2) "Victorian GMRC"
description:
Anomaly #: GCC-789

Safety Class Designation: MCR (Minor Containment Risk)

Special Containment Procedures:

GCC-789 is to be contained within a standard humanoid containment cell at Containment Site-██. The cell is to be furnished with standard amenities, including a bed, desk, and restroom. GCC-789 (formerly Alice Darlington) is permitted access to reading materials, non-electronic entertainment, and approved personal items, specifically etiquette manuals, novels by the Brontë sisters, and embroidery supplies.

Dietary requirements for GCC-789 are to reflect those of a high-status Victorian lady. Meals are to be prepared using ingredients and methods consistent with 19th-century British cuisine. This includes, but is not limited to: roasted meats (beef, lamb, poultry), seasonal vegetables (prepared simply), light broths, custards, and fruit compotes. Personnel are to ensure all provided meals adhere to strict Victorian-era standards of presentation and composition. Tea, served with milk and sugar or naturally occurring sweeteners such as honey, and fruit preserves, is to be provided twice daily.

Personnel interacting with GCC-789 are to maintain a professional demeanor, adhering to strict Victorian-era etiquette. Discussions regarding modern social customs, particularly those deemed "vulgar" or "improper" by Victorian standards, are to be avoided. Direct physical contact with GCC-789 is prohibited except under controlled experimental conditions and with prior authorization from the Site Director. Following any interaction with GCC-789, personnel must undergo a mandatory psychological evaluation, focusing on adherence to proper decorum and the prevention of any "unseemly" behavior.

In experimental settings, GCC-789 may be subjected to various social interaction tests to assess her ability to create punishment subdimensions based on perceived breaches of etiquette. All experiments involving GCC-789 must be approved by the Level D Research Director and must adhere to established safety protocols. Post-experiment, GCC-789 is to be monitored for any unusual psychological or physiological changes, and also for any breach of her usual, extremely proper, behavior, and any signs of punishment subdimensional manifestation.

Description:

GCC-789 designates an anomalous punishment subdimensional manifestation phenomenon exhibited by GCC-789, a 28-year-old blonde-haired and golden-eyed British female, identifying as Alice Darlington, who appears to be displaced from the Victorian era. GCC-789 possesses the ability to create localized punishment subdimensions, designated GCC-789-1, following perceived breaches of Victorian etiquette.

The size and severity of GCC-789-1 appear directly correlated with the severity of the perceived breach. Minor infractions result in small, localized distortions of reality, such as objects momentarily shifting or flickering, accompanied by auditory hallucinations of Victorian-era reprimands. Major breaches, such as outright rudeness or violence, can result in the creation of larger punishment subdimensions, wherein affected individuals are temporarily displaced into a Victorian-era environment designed to punish their perceived transgressions. These environments often reflect the nature of the breach, and may include mock trials, public shaming, or forced labor.

GCC-789 herself appears unaware of her anomalous ability and exhibits no control over GCC-789-1. She displays an unwavering belief that she is a Victorian lady, and any deviation from Victorian social norms causes her extreme distress. She will often correct staff on their etiquette, and will become very flustered if anyone is being improper. She believes that the modern world is simply a strange and vulgar future, and that she must maintain her proper decorum despite the "strange times".

Addendum 789-1 Discovery Log:

GCC-789 was initially discovered on December 12, 2024, at a themed Victorian tea party in Lond
